claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements provider-specific invocation isolation with tool profile controls and boundary enforcement to prevent cross-provider model leakage and project mutation. Explorer evidence is substantially accurate with minor line number corrections needed.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot (97-108) and diffBoundarySnapshots (110-120) implement git and filesystem walk mutation detection. Line numbers match explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms boundary detection mechanism exists and works as claimed.

evidence.summary: agentToolProfile function (189) normalizes tool profile input. Line range is 188-190 not 188-190 as explorer claimed (still accurate).
evidence.why: Tool profile normalization exists; explorer line reference was correct.

evidence.summary: executionConfig (422-470) prepares provider-specific AI configuration. Explorer line reference is accurate.
evidence.why: Execution config function exists with provider-specific handling.

evidence.summary: Claude configuration block (449-462) clears tool restrictions and sets agent_tool_profile. Line numbers match exactly.
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms Claude-specific boundary configuration.

evidence.summary: Boundary enforcement (515-571) takes snapshot before (515) and diff after (566), blockers propagate (584-596). Range is accurate.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement with blocker propagation verified.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation (31-109) has separate paths: Codex (43-61), custom (63-75), Claude (77-109). Explorer said 41-109, actual is 31-109.
evidence.why: Provider isolation exists with distinct code paths per provider. Minor line number correction needed.

evidence.summary: Codex provider uses exec, --cd, --sandbox, -c, --output-last-message. Line numbers match exactly.

evidence.why: Claude provider has richer tool and session configuration than other providers.

evidence.summary: Test name is 'codex dispatch does not inherit a claude default model or force read-only sandbox'. Explorer quoted test name incorrectly but line numbers accurate.
evidence.why: Test verifies Codex provider isolation; test name needs correction in explorer record.

evidence.summary: Test name is 'claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags and relies on boundary enforcement'. Explorer misquoted name as 'clears tool restrictions'.
evidence.why: Test evidence for Claude boundary behavior; test name was misquoted but behavior is correct.

evidence.summary: Test 'claude full tool profile requires explicit authorization for full semantic mode' verifies allowFullAgentTools gate. Line numbers accurate.
evidence.why: Full semantic mode authorization gate verified.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' (381-414) verifies independent boundary enforcement. Line numbers accurate.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement is independent of evidence quality.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, verifier roles with writes_code: false. Needs verification.
evidence.why: Agent protocols explicitly forbid code writing in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant (16) defines allowed scopes as project, external, user, unknown. Line number exact.
Claude provider has tool restriction flags (--tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools) that evidence dispatch clears (lines 456-458 of evidence-dispatch.ts).
evidence.why: Cross-check verification: Claude has tool flags but dispatch deliberately clears them for boundary mode.

evidence.summary: Test assertions (252-254) verify claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools are empty strings, not null.
evidence.why: Important clarification: dispatch clears tool flags to empty strings, not unset/null.

risks: Provider CLI breaking changes could break invocation isolation if argument contracts change.
Non-git projects might have race conditions in boundary detection if filesystem changes between snapshots.
If tool profile naming drifts between provider and harness (e.g., 'research' vs 'fetch'), boundary could be silently weakened.
Line number references in explorer evidence have minor inaccuracies (provider-adapter.ts 41-109 vs 31-109, test name misquotations).
missing: Verification of DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS in src/demand/router.ts lines 54-77 (explorer claims but independent read needed).

result.notes: Explorer evidence is substantially accurate. All core claims verified independently: boundary detection (97-120), tool profile normalization (188-190), execution config (422-470), Claude configuration (449-462), runtime enforcement (515-571), provider isolation (31-109), provider-specific arguments, and test coverage. Minor corrections needed: provider-adapter.ts starts at line 31 not 41, two test names were misquoted (but behavior described correctly), and router.ts lines 54-77 need independent verification to confirm writes_code: false in protocols. No conflicts found; all core safety mechanisms verified.
